facts.text: Kiamesha Lake’s name comes from a Lenape word meaning “crystal‑clear waters,” echoing the lake’s indigenous heritage.
In the 1930s the Concord Hotel opened as the largest Borscht Belt resort, later becoming the Resorts World Catskills casino in 2018.
The hamlet’s lake once hosted a 3,000‑seat theater that drew stars like Judy Garland and Barbra Streisand during the Borscht Belt era.
Railroads reaching Sullivan County in the late 1800s brought New York City vacationers seeking cooler climates, sparking Kiamesha Lake’s resort boom.
Today the former Gibber Hotel houses Yeshiva Viznitz, reflecting the community’s shift from vacation resort to Hasidic educational center.
Kiamesha Lake sits at 1,384 feet above sea level, surrounded by gently rolling hills that define the Appalachian Plateau landscape.
